rag_rules: Fuse each AGREE annex into the owner scope its contents require: interface contracts with the component type, implementation bindings with the component implementation.
Keep type-owned annexes limited to identifiers visible in the type; do not carry implementation-only subcomponent paths into type scope.
In implementation scope, preserve local declaration context so bindings remain after the structural elements they rely on.
generic_rules: Modify only the annex insertion and preserve existing features, subcomponents, connections, properties, and names.
Use only identifiers already present in the chosen owner scope; do not introduce new declarations during fusion.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content uses the standard annex agree block form.
Type scopes normally carry assume/guarantee/const/eq content, while implementation scopes carry assign-style bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not move implementation-specific content into a type owner or type-level content into an implementation owner.
Do not change the architecture or add placeholder model elements just to make an annex fit.
irrelevant_topics: Diagnostic-specific rules about AGREE references to AADL property names
